_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-7 Pediatric Seizures (Revised 03/31/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-7: Pediatric Seizures — MDHHS Approved 3/31/25, Reviewed 2025
purpose: Assessment and treatment of seizures in pediatric patients (< 14 years).

sections.title: Pediatric Seizure Treatment
points: Follow General Pre-hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Contact Medical Control for focal seizures (seizures limited to one body part)
If actively seizing with generalized tonic-clonic activity: protect patient from injury, maintain airway, and provide supplemental oxygen
Administer midazolam according to MI-MEDIC cards
If MI-MEDIC is unavailable: administer midazolam 0.1 mg/kg IM, maximum single dose 10 mg
If IV is established prior to seizure activity: administer midazolam 0.05 mg/kg IV/IO, maximum single dose 5 mg
After midazolam administration, monitor SpO2, EKG, and waveform capnography per End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol
Consider trauma; if evidence or suspicion of trauma, treat according to the applicable protocol in addition to treating seizures
Check blood glucose; start IV/IO if needed; administer dextrose according to MI-MEDIC when blood glucose is < 60 mg/dL
If unable to start IV, administer glucagon IM/IN if available per MCA selection
If seizure persists 10 minutes after the initial dose of midazolam and correction of low blood glucose, repeat midazolam one time per MCA selection
If seizures persist after the second dose, consider underlying causes and contact Medical Control for further instructions

sections.title: Dextrose — Pediatric Weight-Based Dosing (Blood Glucose < 60 mg/dL)
points: Use MI-MEDIC cards when available — dosing table is backup only when blood glucose is < 60 mg/dL
Pink (3–6 mo, 6–7 kg): 3.25g — D25% 13 mL OR D10% 33 mL
Purple (11–18 mo, 10–11 kg): 5g — D25% 20 mL OR D10% 50 mL
White (3–4 yr, 15–18 kg): 8g — D25% 32 mL OR D10% 80 mL
Orange (7–9 yr, 24–29 kg): 12.5g — D50% 25 mL OR D10% 125 mL
NOTE: D10% 5 mL/kg (0.5 gm/kg) may be used for all ages up to 250 mL; push slowly over 1–2 minutes
To obtain D10%: discard 40 mL from D50 amp, draw up 40 mL NS; to obtain D12.5%: discard 37.5 mL from D50 amp, draw up 37.5 mL NS; to obtain D25%: discard 25 mL from D50 amp, draw up 25 mL NS

sections.title: Glucagon Dosing (When IV/IO Cannot Be Established)
points: Glucagon IM (injectable formulation ONLY — per MCA selection): patients < 5 years: 0.5 mg IM; patients ≥ 5 years: 1 mg IM
Glucagon IN — Baqsimi (FDA-approved IN formulation ONLY — per MCA selection): patients < 5 years: DO NOT ADMINISTER; patients ≥ 5 years: 3 mg IN
Do NOT use injectable glucagon via the IN route
EMS clinicians may assist family/caregivers in administering FDA-approved IN glucagon if prescribed for the patient (regardless of age)

sections.title: Not Actively Seizing
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 4-7: Pediatric Seizures — Not Actively Seizing
points: Monitor and treat known underlying causes when possible
Check blood glucose and treat as outlined above
If blood glucose < 60 mg/dL and patient is altered, able to swallow, and 3 months old or older, administer oral glucose
If blood glucose < 60 mg/dL and patient is not alert, administer dextrose according to MI-MEDIC or the dosing table
Check temperature and refer to Pediatric Fever-Treatment Protocol if applicable
Monitor oxygenation and mental status; administer oxygen to maintain 94% SpO2, including ventilatory support as needed per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol
For respiratory depression with high suspicion of opioid involvement, administer naloxone per Opioid Overdose Treatment and Prevention-Treatment Protocol
Consider trauma; if evidence or suspicion of trauma, treat according to applicable protocol
Keep the environment safe for the patient, padding around the patient if possible

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill
MCA must select whether a single repeat dose of midazolam, given if seizure persists 10 minutes after initial midazolam and correction of low glucose, may be administered pre-radio or post-radio
options: Pre-radio repeat midazolam (without Medical Control contact)
Post-radio repeat midazolam (contact Medical Control prior to repeat dose)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select which provider levels may administer glucagon IM (injectable formulation only): < 5 years = 0.5 mg IM; ≥ 5 years = 1 mg IM
MCA must select which provider levels may administer glucagon 3 mg IN (FDA-approved IN formulation ONLY — DO NOT administer to patients < 5 years of age)

key_drugs.name: Midazolam IM (Pediatric Seizure — Before IV)
key_drugs.indication: Active generalized tonic-clonic seizure — administer IM when MI-MEDIC is unavailable
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity; use caution with respiratory compromise
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC cards first. If seizure persists 10 minutes after initial dose and correction of low glucose, repeat once per MCA selection.

key_drugs.name: Midazolam IV/IO (Pediatric Seizure — With IV)
key_drugs.indication: Active generalized tonic-clonic seizure when IV is established prior to seizure activity and MI-MEDIC is unavailable
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity; use caution with respiratory compromise
key_drugs.notes: May repeat once per MCA selection if seizure persists 10 minutes after initial dose and correction of low glucose.

key_drugs.name: Dextrose IV (Pediatric — Weight-Based)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards or weight/color table — D10% 5 mL/kg (0.5 gm/kg) for all ages up to 250 mL
key_drugs.indication: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL and patient not alert
key_drugs.contraindications: Concentrated D50 avoided in small children — dilute to appropriate concentration per color zone table
key_drugs.side_effects: Hyperglycemia, phlebitis, tissue necrosis with extravasation
key_drugs.notes: Push slowly over 1–2 minutes. Contact Medical Control for repeat doses. Use MI-MEDIC first; weight/color table is backup.

key_drugs.name: Glucagon IM (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: < 5 years: 0.5 mg IM; ≥ 5 years: 1 mg IM (injectable formulation ONLY)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia when IV/IO access cannot be established
key_drugs.contraindications: Injectable formulation must NOT be given intranasally; pheochromocytoma
key_drugs.notes: Do NOT use injectable glucagon via IN route. Use MI-MEDIC when available.

key_drugs.name: Glucagon IN — Baqsimi (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: < 5 years: DO NOT ADMINISTER; ≥ 5 years: 3 mg IN
key_drugs.route: Intranasal (FDA-approved IN formulation ONLY — e.g., Baqsimi)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia ≥ 5 years when IV/IO access cannot be established
key_drugs.contraindications: Age < 5 years — DO NOT ADMINISTER; only FDA-approved IN formulation may be used
key_drugs.notes: EMS clinicians may assist family/caregivers in administering FDA-approved IN glucagon if prescribed for the patient (regardless of age).

deterioration_flags: Active generalized tonic-clonic seizure — protect from injury, maintain airway, provide supplemental oxygen, administer midazolam per MI-MEDIC
Seizure persists 10 minutes after initial midazolam and correction of low blood glucose — repeat midazolam one time per MCA selection
Seizure persists after second midazolam dose — consider underlying causes and contact Medical Control
Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL — IV dextrose per weight/color table (Specialist skill); glucagon IM/IN if no IV (per MCA selection)
Focal seizure — contact Medical Control prior to treatment
Post-ictal respiratory depression — support airway; BVM if needed; consider naloxone if opioid exposure suspected
scope_notes: Seizure precautions, positioning, airway management, SpO2 monitoring: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
Glucagon IM/IN: per MCA selection — age-specific dosing; IN contraindicated < 5 years
Repeat midazolam: per MCA selection (pre-radio or post-radio)
